compiled_state.title: Contract freeze (c): event-schema version constant + unknown-event forward-compat rule
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Summary
Sub-effort (c) of story fatty-cipher-range. The event log is the wire format between clones running DIFFERENT rebar versions; declare it versioned and pin forward compatibility for unknown event types.

## Semantics decision (recorded — resolves the "preserved-and-ignored" ambiguity)
- "Ignored": replay skips unknown event_type values WITHOUT error (this is already the behavior — _processors.py:414 — pin it with a test, including that the ticket remains fully readable).
- "Preserved" means at the FILE level, not state level: unknown-type event FILES must survive untouched. The critical case is COMPACTION: rebar compact must NOT absorb or delete unknown-type events into a SNAPSHOT (else a newer clone's data is destroyed by an older clone's compaction). Verify what ticket-compact.sh does with unknown types today; if it absorbs/deletes them, THAT is the code fix in this ticket.
- NO new VERSION event type, no version negotiation. SCHEMA_VERSION constant in ticket_reducer (new _version.py or __init__) + version declaration in docs/event-schema.md.

## Suggested RED test
tests/interfaces/test_event_schema_forward_compat.py: write an event with event_type=FUTURE_TYPE into a ticket dir -> reduce_ticket succeeds, state readable; then run compaction over that ticket -> the FUTURE_TYPE event file still exists on disk afterward and replay still succeeds. RED if compaction destroys it.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SCHEMA_VERSION constant + versioned docs/event-schema.md
- [ ] Unknown-type replay tolerance pinned by test
- [ ] Compaction proven (or fixed) to preserve unknown-type event files
